'''Sam Williams''' (born 18 March 1991) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|hb}} or {{rlp|fe}} for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). In September 2025 Williams was announced as the new coach of the [[Northern Pride RLFC]] in the Queensland Cup after a poor season from the team.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-26 |title='I want to give it a crack': Could we see this former Raider back in the NRL? |url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/story/9075301/former-canberra-raiders-half-sam-williams-joins-northern-pride/ |access-date=2025-09-26 |website=www.canberratimes.com.au |language=en-au}}</ref>

He previously played for [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] in two separate spells and the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[National Rugby League]],<ref>[http://www.raiders.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display&id=3716&player=sam-williams&team=Raiders – Canberra Raiders<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref> and for the [[Catalans Dragons]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Williams was born in [[Cooma]], New South Wales, Australia.

==National Youth Competition==
A Cooma Colts junior, Williams boarded at St Gregory's College for his secondary schooling and played for the Junior Kangaroos in 2010.

Williams debuted for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[NRL Under-20s|National Youth Competition]] in 2009 and was selected in the team of the year in 2010, he was also previously the National Youth Competition Captain.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

==NRL career==
Williams has played 65 first grade matches for the Canberra Raiders and 4 for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]. He has scored 12 first grade tries. Sam made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] début for the Canberra Raiders against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in round 1 of the [[2011 NRL season]] due to an injury sustained by then Canberra Raiders first choice halfback [[Matt Orford]]. Williams impressed on his début with a strong all round performance that earned him the man of the match award.  In round 6 of the season Orford was again ruled out through injury, giving Williams another opportunity in first grade, in this game against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] Williams was sent to the [[Penalty box#Rugby football|sin bin]] for a professional foul. Later in the season Williams managed to cement his spot as the first choice number of seven.

In July 2013, Williams signed a one-year contract with the St. George Illawarra Dragons starting in 2014.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/dragons-sign-sam-williams-one-one-year-deal-10357/ |title=Dragons sign halfback Sam Williams {{!}} NRL {{!}} Zero Tackle |website=zerotackle.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130715055149/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/dragons-sign-sam-williams-one-one-year-deal-10357 |archive-date=2013-07-15}}</ref>

In June 2014, after only being selected for 4 games in 13 rounds for the St. George Illawarra Dragons, Williams joined the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] on a 1-year contract effective immediately.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/articles-9/91-2843-catalans-swoop-for-sam-williams/|title = Dragons Catalans › News › 2014 › June › Catalans swoop for Sam Williams}}</ref>

He returned to the [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] in 2015 on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/sam-williams-returns-to-canberra-raiders-20140918-10ijxb.html|title=Sam Williams returns to Canberra Raiders on two-year deal|date=18 September 2014}}</ref>

In round 2 of the [[2016 NRL season]], Williams wore a special James Bond themed jersey with the number 007 on the back. He would kick the match winning field goal in that game with the Canberra club winning 21–20.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/canberra-raiders-v-roosters--main-story---20160312-gnh9nb.html|title=Sam Williams field goal pilots Canberra Raiders to win over Sydney Roosters|date=12 March 2016}}</ref>

In September 2016, Williams was named at halfback in the 2016 [[NSW Cup|Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] Team of the Year.

Later in 2016, Sam rejected a new contract offer with the Canberra and signed a one-year contract with English Super League club [[Wakefield Trinity]] for the 2017 season,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37507213|title=Sam Williams: Canberra Raiders half-back joins Wakefield Trinity for 2017|publisher=BBC Sport|date=29 September 2016|accessdate=24 January 2018}}</ref> after which he re-joined Canberra on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/canberra-raiders-sign-halfback-sam-williams-for-nrl-and-green-machine-comeback-20171010-gyxt9q.html|title=Canberra Raiders sign halfback Sam Williams for NRL and Green Machine comeback|work=The Canberra Times|date=10 October 2017|accessdate=24 January 2018}}</ref>

Williams made 10 appearances for Canberra in the [[2019 NRL season]] but spent most of the year playing for Canberra's feeder club side [[Mount Pritchard Mounties|Mounties]] in the [[NSW Cup|Canterbury Cup NSW]] competition. Williams played in Mounties elimination final loss against [[Newtown Jets|Newtown]] at [[Campbelltown Sports Stadium|Campbelltown Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/draw/nsw-cup/2019/finals-week-1/mounties-v-jets/|title=Finals week 1|website=NSWRL}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/05/02/sezer-willing-to-wait-in-the-wings/|title=Sezer willing to wait in the wings|publisher=National Rugby League|date=May 2019}}</ref>

On 5 November 2019, Williams signed a two-year contract extension with Canberra.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/11/05/raiders-re-sign-williams-as-sezer-exit-looms/|title=Raiders re-sign Williams as Sezer exit looms|publisher=National Rugby League|date=4 November 2019}}</ref>

In the [[2020 NRL season]] he made a larger impact on Canberra, captaining them to a win in the final game of the regular season against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{Citation|title=Sharks v Raiders – Round 20|date=26 September 2020 |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/09/24/sharks-v-raiders---round-20/|language=en|access-date=2020-09-30}}</ref>

In round 17 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he scored two tries for Canberra in a 30–16 victory over an Origin depleted Manly side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-ricky-stuart-canberra-raiders-vs-manly-sea-eagles-josh-hodgson-jarrod-croker-round-17-score-result-titans-loss/news-story/471905f0cc3a5e3ee1dfea51b40a1277/|title='His best week of the year': Ricky's new approach that sparked Raiders turnaround|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=9 July 2021}}</ref>

In round 15 of the [[2022 NRL season]], it was announced Williams would leave the [[Canberra Raiders]] having been unable to make it back into the top grade side. Williams played 8 games, scored 3 tries and kicked 15 goals for the Raiders [[NSW Cup]] side that year.

== Post Playing ==
Williams retired from playing in 2022 and went on to become the captain-coach of the Queanbeyan Kangaroos in the Canberra Raiders Cup.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://the-riotact.com/former-raider-sam-williams-open-to-nrl-coaching-after-queanbeyan-kangaroos-success/703181 | title=Former Raider Sam Williams open to NRL coaching after Queanbeyan Kangaroos' success }}</ref> On 25 September 2025, [[Northern Pride RLFC]] announced that Williams would become their coach for the 2026-27 seasons.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-26 |title='I want to give it a crack': Could we see this former Raider back in the NRL? |url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/story/9075301/former-canberra-raiders-half-sam-williams-joins-northern-pride/ |access-date=2025-09-26 |website=www.canberratimes.com.au |language=en-au}}</ref>
